question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the Spring Data JPA transaction for findByAlias() to be suspended mid-execution, and when the connection is re-established, the query re-executes against a database state where a concurrent write has changed the alias column, causing findByAlias() to return a different entity ID than the one that was valid at the start of the record-file processing, introducing a TOCTOU inconsistency in entity resolution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes findByEvmAddress() to be called with a null byte array (due to upstream null propagation during partition-induced error handling), causing the native SQL query to execute 'evm_address = NULL' which always evaluates to false in SQL, silently returning empty Optional and causing the entity to be permanently unresolvable without any error being raised?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the Iterable<Long> ids parameter passed to updateContractType() to be partially consumed before the partition occurs, and since the native query uses ':ids' binding with Spring Data JPA's Iterable expansion, a partial iteration could result in the SQL being constructed with an incomplete IN clause, causing a SQL syntax error that rolls back the transaction and leaves all entity types unchanged?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the native query 'select evm_address,id from entity where id in (?1) and length(evm_address) > 0' to execute against a database node mid-migration (AccountEvmAddressMigration running), returning a mix of rows with and without evm_address populated, causing the Caffeine cache to store null/empty EVM addresses for entities whose migration has not yet completed, permanently poisoning the cache for those entities?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the @Transactional class-level annotation to propagate an existing transaction context into findByAlias(), and when the outer transaction is rolled back due to the partition, the read result from findByAlias() is discarded but the in-memory cache in EntityIdServiceImpl has already been populated with the (now-invalid) entity ID, causing subsequent lookups to use a stale cached value that no longer reflects the committed database state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the importer to lose its database connection after updateContractType() sends the UPDATE statement but before receiving the acknowledgment, causing the importer to retry the entire record-file processing, and since the UPDATE is idempotent for already-CONTRACT rows but not for rows that were concurrently deleted, the retry could attempt to set type='CONTRACT' on a deleted entity, violating the deletion invariant?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes findByEvmAddress() to execute against a database where the entity table index on evm_address is being rebuilt (due to partition-induced crash recovery), causing the query to perform a full table scan that times out, throwing a QueryTimeoutException that propagates through the @Transactional boundary and crashes the importer's processing thread?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes findEvmAddressesByIds() to be called with an empty Iterable (due to upstream state corruption from the partition), and since the native query 'id in (?1)' with an empty collection causes a SQL syntax error in some JDBC drivers, this could throw an uncaught exception that crashes the SyntheticLogListener.onEnd() handler and prevents record file completion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the database connection used by findByAlias() to be returned to the connection pool in a broken state (TCP connection silently dropped), and when the next caller acquires this connection, the @Transactional annotation does not validate the connection before use, causing the next findByAlias() or updateContractType() call to fail with a broken pipe error, corrupting the transaction state for that record file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes a split-brain scenario where two importer instances (if horizontally scaled) both call updateContractType() with overlapping ID sets simultaneously, and since the UPDATE uses 'type <> CONTRACT' as a guard but not a row-level lock, both updates could race and one could overwrite a concurrent delete, causing a deleted entity to be re-typed as CONTRACT?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the database to return a connection error specifically during the result set fetch phase of findByEvmAddress() (after the query is sent but before results are received), causing Spring Data JPA to throw a JpaSystemException wrapping a HibernateException, which is not handled by EntityIdServiceImpl.cacheLookup()'s Cache.ValueRetrievalException catch block, propagating uncaught and crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the Caffeine LoadingCache in SyntheticLogListener to invoke the CacheLoader.loadAll() method, which calls findEvmAddressesByIds(), and when the DB is unreachable, the CacheLoader throws an exception that Caffeine propagates to all waiting threads as a CacheLoadingException, causing all concurrent SyntheticLogListener.onEnd() calls to fail simultaneously and halting all record file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes findByAlias() to execute against a database node that has diverged from the primary (split-brain), where the alias column has been updated to point to a new entity ID on the primary but the replica still returns the old ID, causing the importer to cache the old entity ID and associate all subsequent transactions with the wrong account for the duration of the partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the @Modifying annotation's clearAutomatically behavior to fail (since the EntityManager cannot flush to the database), causing the first-level cache (Hibernate session cache) to hold stale entity objects with the old type, and subsequent findById() calls within the same transaction to return the stale non-CONTRACT type, causing downstream logic to make incorrect decisions based on the wrong entity type?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the database to be temporarily unavailable during the importer's startup phase, causing findByEvmAddress() to fail for all EVM address lookups during initial record file processing, and since the EntityIdServiceImpl cache is never populated during this window, all entity-to-EVM-address associations for that period are permanently lost from the mirror node's state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes findEvmAddressesByIds() to return results from a database node that has not yet applied the latest evm_address updates (replication lag during partition recovery), causing the Caffeine cache to be populated with stale null evm_address values for entities whose evm_address was just set, and since the cache TTL is long, these entities will have incorrect EVM address mappings for an extended period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the native SQL query in findByAlias() to be executed with a byte[] alias parameter that was serialized before the partition and deserialized after reconnection with a different encoding, causing the binary comparison 'alias = ?1' to fail to match the correct entity, returning empty Optional and causing the importer to fail to resolve the alias even though the entity exists in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the Spring transaction synchronization to fail during updateContractType(), causing the transaction to be marked for rollback-only, but since the @Transactional annotation on the interface does not specify rollbackFor, the transaction manager may commit a partial state if the rollback-only flag is not properly propagated, leaving some entity types updated and others not?
